_comment: Standard CARC (Claim Adjustment Reason Code) denial codes with appeal pathways. CO=Contractual Obligation, PR=Patient Responsibility, OA=Other Adjustment.

CO-50.description: These are non-covered services because this is not deemed a medical necessity by the payer
CO-50.appeal_pathway: Level 1 Administrative Appeal → Level 2 External Review (if denied)
tips: Cite specific LCD/NCD policy criteria that are met
Include clinical literature supporting medical necessity
Request peer-to-peer review with medical director
Attach all supporting clinical documentation not included in original submission

CO-97.description: The benefit for this service is included in the payment/allowance for another service/procedure that has already been adjudicated
tips: Review CPT bundling rules (CCI edits)
Submit corrected claim with appropriate modifier (-59, -XU, -XS, -XP, -XE)
P2P not applicable — this is a billing/coding issue, not clinical

CO-4.description: The service/equipment/drug is not covered/allowed by this payer/plan
CO-4.appeal_pathway: Coverage Exception Request or Plan Change
tips: Verify correct payer and plan ID submitted
Check plan benefit document for coverage exclusions
If excluded: submit coverage exception citing clinical urgency or lack of alternatives

CO-16.description: Claim/service lacks information or has submission/billing error(s)
CO-16.appeal_pathway: Corrected Claim or Additional Documentation Submission
tips: Review claim for missing fields (diagnosis pointer, modifier, NPI)
Resubmit with complete information
Attach EOB with remark codes for context

PR-96.description: Non-covered charge(s). At least one Remark Code must be provided
PR-96.appeal_pathway: Coverage Exception Request or Patient Financial Assistance
tips: Review remark codes (CARC/RARC) for specific exclusion reason
If medically necessary and excluded: submit coverage exception with supporting literature
Consider patient assistance programs for pharmacy denials

CO-57.description: Revenue code and Procedure code do not match
tips: Verify revenue code matches CPT/HCPCS according to CMS revenue code-to-procedure crosswalk
Submit corrected UB-04 with corrected revenue code
Confirm original claim ICN and date of service
If truly a duplicate: void the duplicate
If not a duplicate (different DOS, different service): resubmit with proof

CO-151.description: Payment adjusted because the payer deems the information submitted does not support this level of service
CO-151.appeal_pathway: Level 1 Administrative Appeal with additional clinical documentation
tips: Attach complete clinical notes supporting level of service billed
Reference MDM documentation for E&M services
Consider P2P if level of service is clearly justified clinically
